items.title: Polyplot
items.text:
Polyplot is a Three.js (React Three Fiber) anywidget for 2.5D polygon stacks: it preprocesses stacked 2D outlines per cell_id and ZIndex, builds triangulated meshes, exports GLB tiles, and serves them to a WebGL viewer in the browser (marimo, Jupyter, and similar).

  • Get started – install, sample data, and the quickstart notebook.
  • User guide – meshify, plot, and caching.
  • PyPI / source – uv sync from the repository root; API docstrings in polyplot/.

items.title: At a glance
items.text:
Piece Role meshify(gdf, ...) Preprocess, tile, and write tiles.json and GLB shards under a content-hashed directory (default .polyplot/) plot(gdf, ...) Ensure tiles exist, run a local tile server, and return a marimo anywidget WebGL view

The viewer controls wireframe, opacity, and background in the UI; they are not set from Python.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• Python 3.12 or newer
  • uv recommended (see the repo pyproject.toml for direct dependencies)

items.title: Install
items.text:
git clone https://github.com/ckmah/polyplot.git
cd polyplot
uv sync

items.title: Sample data
items.text:
The repository includes a small sample_data/liver_crop_sample.parquet (about 50 cells in one spatial patch) for demos and CI. To build a new subset from a local full liver_crop.parquet, use:

uv run python scripts/make_liver_subset.py

Larger local parquets in sample_data/ are not tracked by git (see sample_data/README.md in the repository).

items.title: Quick start (marimo)
items.text:
The shortest path is quickstart.py at the repository root (marimo). From a clone of the repository:

uv run marimo edit quickstart.py

A “Open in molab” badge in the README links to a hosted run once the file is on the default branch.

items.title: Full demo
items.text:
For a longer walkthrough, see notebook.py:

uv run marimo edit notebook.py

items.title: Build this documentation site locally
items.text:
uvx zensical serve

Open the URL printed in the terminal (by default http://127.0.0.1:8000).

items.title: Publish
items.text: Documentation for this site is built with Zensical in CI and published to GitHub Pages at the site_url configured in zensical.toml.

items.title: Data model
items.text:
polyplot expects a GeoDataFrame with at least:

  • cell_id – stable identifier for each biological cell (or object).
  • ZIndex – ordering along the stack (height or slice).
  • geometry – 2D polygons in a consistent map CRS.

Each row is one 2D outline; multiple rows per cell_id with different ZIndex form the 2.5D stack.

items.title: meshify
items.text:
import geopandas as gpd
import polyplot as po

gdf = gpd.read_parquet("sample_data/liver_crop_sample.parquet")
info = po.meshify(
    gdf,
    out_dir=".polyplot",
    smooth=True,
    use_cache=True,
    show_progress=True,
)
  • Writes **tiles.json** and per-tile GLB files under a content-hashed subfolder of out_dir (default .polyplot).
  • smooth – 3D Taubin smoothing (on) or raw surfaces (off).
  • use_cache – if the same fingerprint was built before, reuses the cache and sets _cache_hit in the return value.
  • show_progress – in marimo, a progress display while building.

items.title: plot
items.text:
po.plot(gdf, use_cache=True, max_concurrent_fetches=4)

Starts or reuses a local tile server and returns a marimo anywidget with a Three.js / WebGL view. The toolbar adjusts wireframe, opacity, and background.

Additional optional controls:

# On-demand tile streaming: bound what can load by distance cap.
po.plot(gdf, on_demand=True, max_orbit_distance=250.0)

# Clamp how far you can zoom out (max distance from orbit target).
po.plot(gdf, max_orbit_distance=250.0)

items.title: Caching and disk use
items.text: meshify keeps a content-addressed cache. Older digests under the same out_dir can be pruned; the set used by an active plot server is kept.

items.title: Optional tooling
items.text: • gltfpack on your PATH can help shrink GLB output; meshify enables compression when available.
